title: cephaelis tontaneoides
intro: Cephaelis tontaneoides, also known as Wild Ipecac, is a plant species native to Central and South America. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds. The plant contains a compound called emetine, which has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and fever. Wild Ipecac can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried root extract, and has been used in traditional medicine for its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory congestion.
